name: Harlequin
summary: High-contrast pattern morph with bold markings covering the legs and climbing high up the dorsum.

name: Extreme Harlequin
summary: The top tier of harlequin pattern — high-coverage, high-contrast, often with solid cream flanks and legs.

name: Pinstripe
summary: Raised cream scales running along the dorsal ridge from neck to tail base.

name: Phantom Pinstripe
summary: Raised dorsal scales that remain dark (unpigmented cream) while the flanks carry bright pattern — a reversed look.

name: Dalmatian
summary: Discrete dark spots scattered across the body — black is most common, red and occasionally white also occur.

name: Super Dalmatian
summary: Extreme dalmatian with 100+ well-defined spots covering nearly the entire body.

name: Flame
summary: A pattern type with color restricted to the dorsum — flanks stay a solid base color.

name: Tiger
summary: Horizontal dark banding across the dorsum, running perpendicular to the spine.

name: Brindle
summary: Heavier, more irregular banding than tiger — often described as "broken" or "marbled" tiger.

name: Extreme Brindle
summary: Dominant, coverage-heavy brindle with dark pattern consuming most of the dorsum and flanks.

name: Tiger / Brindle
summary: Intermediate expression between tiger and brindle — sold as a single "T/B" category in much of the hobby.

name: Super Soft Scale
summary: Homozygous form of soft scale — dramatically reduced scales and a near-leather appearance.

name: White Wall
summary: Structural trait producing a bright white flank that does not fire down — hence "wall of white."

name: White Wall White Spot
summary: Variant of white wall characterized by isolated bright white spots rather than a solid wall.

name: Cappuccino
summary: Structural pattern trait creating a distinctive "saddle" mark on the back with a clean dorsal contrast.

name: Frappuccino
summary: Homozygous form of cappuccino — exaggerated saddle, muted pattern, and a distinctive pale body tone.

name: Lilly White
summary: Proven incomplete-dominant trait producing bold white markings — the super form is lethal.

name: Hypo (Hypomelanistic)
summary: Line-bred trait reducing black pigment — warmer, brighter animals with pale bellies and reduced dark markings.

name: Patternless
summary: Line-bred trait for solid-colored animals with no discernible pattern — often the base for breeding projects.

name: Moonglow
summary: Line-bred ultra-pale, near-white patternless animal — essentially a "super-cream" patternless with no visible markings.

name: Translucent
summary: Line-bred trait producing a degree of skin translucency — organs and bone structure faintly visible.

name: Orange Base
summary: Polygenic base color in warm orange tones — common and widely produced.

name: Yellow Base
summary: Polygenic base color with bright yellow ground color — common and beginner-friendly.

name: Olive
summary: Polygenic base color producing a distinctive olive-green or khaki tone.

name: Chocolate
summary: Polygenic base color with rich deep-brown tones — like dark chocolate.

name: Lavender
summary: Polygenic base color showing an unusual purple-gray tone — rare and sought-after.

name: Buckskin
summary: Warm tan or leather-colored base — a classic "starter" color with pleasant earthy tones.

name: Tricolor
summary: Three-color combination: dark base + cream pattern + a third distinct color (usually red or orange).

name: Bicolor
summary: Animal with two clear contrasting colors — typically a base plus one pattern color.
